name: Kids Wetsuits
meta_description: Buy kids' wetsuits — 2mm to 5mm, warm & stretchy. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Kids Wetsuits for Every Water Temperature

Getting kids comfortable in the water starts with the right wetsuit. A suit that's too cold cuts sessions short; one that's too tight kills enthusiasm before it starts. Our kids wetsuit range covers every water temperature from warm Mediterranean summers to cold Atlantic winters, in sizes from age 4 to 16.

We stock ION kids wetsuits across the full thickness range — 2mm shorties for summer paddling, 3/2mm full suits for spring and autumn sessions, and 4/3mm models with sealed seams for cold-water surfing and kiteboarding. Every suit uses SuperFlex neoprene that stretches with growing bodies and pre-shaped knees that allow unrestricted movement on a board.

Choosing the Right Kids Wetsuit

Wetsuit thickness should match water temperature, not air temperature. For water above 20°C, a 2mm shorty is sufficient. Between 16–20°C, a 3/2mm full suit keeps kids warm for a full session. Below 16°C, move to a 4/3mm with sealed seams and consider adding gloves and boots.

Sizing is critical for kids wetsuits — too loose and cold water flushes through; too tight and the child won't want to wear it. ION sizes by height in cm (92–164cm), making it straightforward to match the right suit to your child.

Why Buy Kids Wetsuits at Surf Store

Our team can advise on thickness, size, and entry style for your child's age, height, and local water temperature. We stock a wide range of sizes and can help you avoid the most common mistake in kids wetsuit buying — sizing up too aggressively.

FAQ

Q: How long will a kids wetsuit last?
A: Typically one to two seasons depending on growth. Buy for current size, not future growth — a loose wetsuit doesn't insulate.

Q: Back-zip or chest-zip for kids?
A: Back-zip is easier for children to get in and out of independently. We recommend back-zip for ages under 12.

Q: Can kids wetsuits be used for swimming pool lessons?
A: Yes — a 2mm shorty works well for pool sessions in cooler facilities.

name: Men Wetsuits
meta_description: Buy men's wetsuits — 2mm to 6mm, shorties & full suits. ION, Mystic, O'Neill. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Men's Wetsuits — From Summer Shorties to Winter Full Suits

A good wetsuit is the single most important purchase for any water sports rider. Get it right and you ride longer, more comfortably, and with better performance. Get it wrong and you spend every session thinking about the cold instead of your technique. Our men's wetsuit range covers the full spectrum from 2mm summer vests to 6/5mm hooded winter suits.

We carry ION wetsuits as our primary brand — offering the best balance of thermal performance, flexibility, and durability in European water sports. ION's Element series delivers reliable warmth at a mid-range price, the Seek series adds premium features like chest-zip entry and smooth-skin panels, and the Seek Amp is the warmest suit available for extreme cold-water sessions.

Choosing Your Wetsuit Thickness

Match thickness to water temperature, not season or air temperature:

  • 2mm: Water 22°C+ (Mediterranean summer, tropical)
  • 3/2mm: Water 16–22°C (spring/autumn, mild coastal)
  • 4/3mm: Water 12–16°C (northern Europe spring/autumn)
  • 5/4mm: Water 8–12°C (winter in central Europe)
  • 6/5mm with hood: Water below 8°C (winter in northern Europe)

Entry Style: Back-Zip vs Chest-Zip

Back-zip suits are easier to get on and off — ideal for beginners and casual riders. Chest-zip suits are warmer (no zip running down the spine where cold water enters) and fit more snugly — preferred by serious riders in cold water. Front-zip suits offer a middle ground.

Why Buy Men's Wetsuits at Surf Store

We stock ION men's wetsuits from XS to XXXL across all thicknesses and entry styles. Our team uses ION wetsuits daily and can advise on size, thickness, and model for your body type, sport, and local conditions. In-store fitting available in Maribor.

FAQ

Q: How tight should a wetsuit be?
A: Snug everywhere with no air pockets — especially lower back and armpits. You should be able to breathe comfortably but feel light compression on your torso.

Q: How do I care for my wetsuit?
A: Rinse in fresh water after every use, dry inside-out in shade, store on a wide hanger or flat. Never expose to direct sun or heat.

Q: When should I replace my wetsuit?
A: When seams leak consistently, neoprene loses stretch, or the suit no longer keeps you warm for a full session — typically after 2–4 seasons with regular use.

name: Neoprene Accessories
meta_title: Neoprene Accessories — Gloves, Hoods & More | surf-store.com
meta_description: Buy neoprene accessories — gloves, hoods, rashguards, surf shoes & thermal shirts. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Neoprene Accessories — Complete Your Cold-Water Setup

Your wetsuit is only as warm as its weakest point. Exposed hands, feet, and head account for the majority of heat loss during cold-water sessions. Neoprene accessories close those gaps — turning a 30-minute shiver into a full-length session in comfort. Our range covers gloves, hoods, boots, shoes, rashguards, and thermal base layers from ION.

Neoprene Gloves

We stock gloves from 2mm for mild conditions to 4/3mm lobster-claw mitts for winter use. ION Claw gloves offer the best balance between warmth and dexterity — the split-finger design keeps hands warm while allowing bar and boom grip. For extreme cold, the ION Arctic gloves provide maximum insulation.

Hoods & Beanies

A neoprene hood is essential below 12°C water temperature. ION hoods come in 2mm and 3mm thicknesses with adjustable face openings and smooth-skin seals that prevent flushing. For milder days, a neoprene beanie provides head warmth without restricting vision.

Boots & Surf Shoes

Neoprene boots protect feet from cold, rocks, and reef. We carry round-toe boots for general water sports use and split-toe boots for board sports where footstrap feel matters. Thicknesses range from 1.5mm reef walkers to 6mm winter boots.

Rashguards & Thermal Layers

UV rashguards protect against sunburn during summer sessions. Thermal tops layer under wetsuits in winter to add warmth without adding bulk. ION's Thermo Top adds approximately 2°C of effective warmth to any wetsuit.

Why Buy Neoprene Accessories at Surf Store

We stock the full ION accessories range with expert advice on sizing and thickness for your conditions.

FAQ

Q: What thickness gloves for 14°C water?
A: 3mm gloves are comfortable at 14°C. Below 10°C, move to 4/3mm or lobster-claw mitts.

Q: Can I wear boots inside footstraps?
A: Yes — use split-toe boots for better strap feel, and adjust straps slightly wider to accommodate the boot.

Q: Do I need a hood with a hooded wetsuit?
A: No — hooded wetsuits (5/4mm, 6/5mm) include an integrated hood. Standalone hoods pair with non-hooded suits.

name: Women Wetsuits
meta_description: Buy women's wetsuits — 2mm to 6mm, cut for women. ION, Mystic, O'Neill. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Women's Wetsuits — Designed for Women Riders

Women's wetsuits are not just smaller men's suits. The best women's wetsuits are cut on female-specific patterns — shorter torso length, adjusted chest and hip panels, narrower shoulders, and higher back panels that prevent flushing when riding in a forward-leaning stance on foil boards and kiteboards.

Our women's wetsuit range centres on the ION Amaze series — ION's dedicated women's line that spans the full thickness range from 2mm summer suits to 5/4mm winter models. Every Amaze suit features anatomically adjusted neoprene panels, pre-bent knees, and soft-touch collar linings that eliminate neck rash during long sessions.

Choosing Your Women's Wetsuit

Thickness follows the same water temperature guide as men's suits: 2mm for 22°C+, 3/2mm for 16–22°C, 4/3mm for 12–16°C, and 5/4mm below 12°C. Women tend to feel cold faster than men in water, so we often recommend going one step thicker than expected — especially for longer sessions.

The ION Amaze Element is our most popular women's suit — offering excellent warmth and stretch at a mid-range price in both back-zip and front-zip options. The Amaze Amp adds premium features: smooth-skin chest panels, thermal lining, and minimal seam construction for maximum flexibility.

Why Buy Women's Wetsuits at Surf Store

We carry ION women's wetsuits from XS to XL across all thicknesses. Our team can advise on sizing — ION uses height/weight charts with specific measurements for bust and hip that ensure a secure, comfortable fit. In-store fitting available in Maribor.

FAQ

Q: Can women wear men's wetsuits?
A: It's possible but not recommended. Men's suits don't account for female body proportions — the result is poor fit, cold spots, and discomfort around the chest and hips.

Q: What entry style for women's wetsuits?
A: Front-zip or back-zip both work well. Front-zip is warmer; back-zip is easier for getting dressed alone. Chest-zip suits are available in premium models.

Q: Does ION make maternity wetsuits?
A: ION doesn't produce a specific maternity range. For pregnancy, sizing up in a stretchy model like the Amaze Element often works for early stages. Consult your doctor before water sports during pregnancy.

name: Windsurf Accessories
meta_description: Buy windsurf accessories — fins, bags & spare parts. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
meta_keywords: windsurf accessories,windsurf fins,windsurf spare parts,sail bag
description_html:
Windsurf Accessories — Fins, Bags & Spare Parts

The right accessories make the difference between a smooth session and a frustrating one. A worn-out fin costs you upwind angle. A missing extension base means you can't rig your sail. A quality board bag protects your investment during transport. Our windsurf accessories range covers everything you need beyond the board and sail — from performance fins to replacement hardware and protective storage.

Fins

We stock freeride, slalom, and wave fins in US Box, Tuttle Box, and Deep Tuttle fittings. Fin choice directly affects board speed, upwind ability, and control in chop. Larger fins (36–46cm) provide power and stability for freeride; smaller fins (18–28cm) give manoeuvrability for wave sailing. Carbon fins offer the best performance; fibreglass fins provide durability at a lower price.

Bags & Storage

Board bags protect against UV damage, dings, and scratches during transport and storage. We carry single-board bags for daily use and multi-board travel bags with wheel systems and padding for air travel. Sail bags, mast bags, and quiver bags complete the range.

Spare Parts & Hardware

Downhaul ropes, uphaul lines, harness lines, mast bases, extension systems, universal joints, and footstrap screws — we stock the small parts that keep your rig functional. Our recommendation: carry a basic spare parts kit in your car to avoid session-ending equipment failures.

Why Buy Windsurf Accessories at Surf Store

We stock accessories from Duotone, NeilPryde, and third-party manufacturers with expert advice on compatibility.

FAQ

Q: What fin size for a 130L freeride board?
A: A 38–42cm freeride fin suits most 130L boards. Go larger for light wind, smaller for strong wind and control.

Q: Do I need a padded board bag?
A: For car transport and home storage, a basic day bag is sufficient. For travel or shipping, invest in a padded bag with 10mm+ foam.

Q: What harness line length should I use?
A: 26–30" for most adult riders. Shorter lines give more control; longer lines provide more comfort and leverage.

name: Windsurf Foils
meta_description: Buy windsurf foils — complete hydrofoil sets for foil windsurfing. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Windsurf Foils — Fly Above the Water

Windsurf foiling has transformed light-wind sailing. Where traditional windsurfing requires 15+ knots to plane, a hydrofoil gets you flying in 8–12 knots — turning marginal days into full sessions. Our windsurf foil range includes complete foil sets, individual components, and dedicated foil boards from Duotone, Fanatic, and NeilPryde.

Complete Foil Sets vs Components

If you're new to foil windsurfing, start with a complete foil set — front wing, stabiliser, mast, fuselage, and mounting hardware matched and tested together. The Duotone Spirit foil system and NeilPryde Glide are our top recommendations for beginners. Experienced foilers can mix components — swap front wings for speed or lift characteristics, change mast length for conditions, and fine-tune stabiliser angle.

Choosing Your First Windsurf Foil

Start with a larger front wing (1200–1800cm²) for early liftoff and stable flight. Use a shorter mast (60–70cm) to reduce the consequences of crashes while learning. An aluminium mast saves money during the learning phase when crashes are frequent — upgrade to carbon once your technique is solid.

Pair your foil with a dedicated foil board (90–120L) or a freeride board with a Deep Tuttle or foil-specific box. Foil sails like the Duotone Warp_Foil are optimised for foiling with reduced power delivery and a shorter luff.

Why Buy Windsurf Foils at Surf Store

Our team can advise on foil setup for your weight, sail size, and experience level. We build complete foil packages with matched components and offer individual wings and masts for upgrading existing setups.

FAQ

Q: Can I foil with my existing windsurf board?
A: Some freeride boards accept foils via a Deep Tuttle box. However, a dedicated foil board with lower volume and a centred stance offers much better control.

Q: How difficult is windsurf foiling to learn?
A: Expect 5–10 sessions to achieve stable flight if you're already an intermediate windsurfer. Previous foiling experience in other disciplines helps significantly.

Q: What mast length for a beginner foiler?
A: Start with 60–70cm. Move to 85–95cm once you can maintain stable flight and handle takeoffs and touchdowns smoothly.

name: Windsurf Gear
meta_title: Windsurf Rig Components — Masts, Booms & More | surf-store.com
meta_description: Buy windsurf rig parts — masts, booms, extensions & bases. Neilpryde, Duotone & more. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Windsurf Rig Components — Masts, Booms, Extensions & Bases

A windsurf rig is only as good as its weakest component. Your sail's designed shape relies on the correct mast bend curve, the boom needs to hold its length under load, and the extension must match your sail's luff length precisely. Our windsurf gear section covers every rig component from Duotone, NeilPryde, and Gaastra.

Masts

Masts come in SDM (Standard Diameter) and RDM (Reduced Diameter) formats, with carbon content from 30% to 100%. Higher carbon percentage means lighter weight and faster power response — critical for racing and high-wind wave sailing. RDM masts are preferred for wave sails and smaller sails where the mast needs to flex during impacts. Always match your mast to your sail's published IMCS rating and luff length.

Booms

Adjustable booms cover a range of sail sizes — a 140–200cm boom handles most freeride sails from 5.5m to 8.0m. Carbon booms are significantly lighter than aluminium, reducing arm fatigue over long sessions. Boom height and harness line position directly affect your riding stance and comfort.

Extensions & Bases

Mast extensions bridge the gap between your mast length and sail luff length. Standard extensions adjust from 30cm to 46cm. Universal joints (UJ) connect the rig to the board and must be maintained — replace rubber UJ components annually to prevent breakage. Mast bases with integrated pulleys make downhauling easier.

Why Buy Windsurf Gear at Surf Store

We stock masts, booms, extensions, and bases from Duotone, NeilPryde, and Gaastra with expert compatibility advice. Our team can verify that your mast matches your sail and your boom covers your size range.

FAQ

Q: Can I use one mast for multiple sails?
A: Yes — if the sails share the same luff length and recommended IMCS rating. This is common within a brand's range.

Q: What boom length do I need?
A: Check your sail's recommended boom length — it's printed on the sail. Most freeride riders cover their quiver with one adjustable boom.

Q: How often should I replace my universal joint?
A: Inspect the rubber annually. Replace if cracked, brittle, or deformed — a failed UJ means losing your rig on the water.

name: Windsurf Harnesses
meta_description: Buy windsurf harnesses — waist & seat, plus spreader bars, hooks & parts. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Windsurf Harnesses — Waist & Seat Styles

A harness transfers the sail's pull from your arms to your body weight — letting you ride longer and with more power than arm strength alone allows. Choosing the right harness type, size, and hook position affects your comfort, stance, and ability to control the rig in strong wind. We stock ION windsurf harnesses in waist and seat designs.

Waist Harness vs Seat Harness

Waist harnesses sit around your lower rib cage and provide maximum freedom of movement. They're preferred by wave sailors, freestylers, and advanced freeride riders who need to shift their body position dynamically. The downside: they can ride up under heavy load if not fitted correctly.

Seat harnesses wrap around the hips and upper thighs, distributing load across a wider area. They're more comfortable for long freeride sessions and recommended for beginners because they stay in position without adjustment. The trade-off is less rotational freedom for radical sailing styles.

Spreader Bars & Hooks

The spreader bar connects your harness to the harness lines on the boom. Standard hooks suit most riders; sliding hooks allow vertical adjustment during sailing. Spreader bar width should match your torso — too narrow concentrates pressure; too wide limits arm movement.

Why Buy Windsurf Harnesses at Surf Store

Harness fit is highly personal. Our team can advise on waist vs seat, size selection, and hook height based on your riding style and body shape. ION harnesses use European sizing with detailed measurement guides.

FAQ

Q: Which harness for a beginner windsurfer?
A: A seat harness — it stays in position, distributes load comfortably, and lets you focus on sailing technique rather than harness adjustment.

Q: How tight should a windsurf harness be?
A: Snug but not restrictive. You should be able to breathe deeply. The harness should not ride up when you hang your weight from the hook.

Q: Can I use a kite harness for windsurfing?
A: Not recommended. Kite and windsurf harnesses have different hook positions, load angles, and spreader bar designs.

name: Windsurf Sails
meta_description: Buy windsurf sails — freeride, wave, freestyle & race. Neilpryde, Duotone, Severne & more. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Windsurf Sails — Freeride, Wave, Race & Foil

The sail is the engine of your windsurf setup. Size, shape, and construction determine how early you plane, how fast you go, how smoothly you transition through gusts, and how much control you retain in strong wind. Our sail range covers every discipline from entry-level freeride to PWA-level slalom racing, with brands including Duotone, NeilPryde, Gaastra, and Point-7.

Sail Types

Freeride sails (5.0–8.5m) offer the widest wind range and easiest handling — ideal for recreational sailing and light-to-moderate wind. Wave sails (3.3–5.5m) are compact, lightweight, and built for manoeuvrability in breaking surf. Race/slalom sails (6.0–9.8m) maximise speed through stiff cams, high-aspect profiles, and efficient power delivery. Foil sails (4.5–8.0m) are optimised for windsurf foiling — narrow heads, moderate power, and early liftoff.

Choosing Your Sail Size

Sail size depends on your weight, local wind average, and board volume. As a general guide for an 80kg freeride rider:

  • 12–18 knots: 7.0–8.0m
  • 18–25 knots: 5.5–6.5m
  • 25+ knots: 4.5–5.5m

A two-sail quiver (e.g., 6.0m + 7.5m) covers most European wind conditions for freeride riders.

Why Buy Windsurf Sails at Surf Store

We stock sails from 3.3m to 9.8m across Duotone, NeilPryde, Gaastra, and Point-7. Our windsurf team can advise on size, mast compatibility, and complete rig packages at bundle pricing. Free EU shipping on orders over €99.

FAQ

Q: Can I use one sail for freeride and wave?
A: Dedicated sails perform better, but a 5.0–5.5m freeride sail can handle small waves if conditions aren't extreme.

Q: How do I know which mast fits my sail?
A: Every sail specifies a recommended mast IMCS rating and luff length. Match these numbers to the mast specifications — our team can verify compatibility.

Q: How long do windsurf sails last?
A: With proper care (rinse, dry, roll not fold), a quality sail lasts 3–5 seasons of regular use. Monofilm panels degrade faster in UV than X-Ply.

name: Wing Accessories
meta_description: Buy wing foil accessories — bags, bladders, leashes & spare parts. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
meta_keywords: wing foil accessories,wing bag,wing bladder,wing leash
description_html:
Wing Foil Accessories — Bags, Leashes, Bladders & More

Wing foiling gear takes a beating — wings get dropped on rocks, bladders get punctured, and leashes wear from constant tension. A well-stocked accessories kit keeps you on the water instead of on the shore. Our wing foil accessories range covers everything from protective bags to replacement bladders, leashes, repair kits, and board protection.

Wing Bags

A quality wing bag protects your wing during transport and storage. We stock padded bags from Duotone and Fanatic that accommodate wings from 3m to 7m+. UV exposure degrades canopy material quickly — always store your wing in a bag when not in use.

Leashes

A wrist leash connects you to your wing during crashes and transitions. Coiled leashes reduce drag in the water; straight leashes are lighter but create more resistance. Always use a wing leash — a dropped wing in wind moves fast and can be dangerous to retrieve without one.

Replacement Bladders & Repair

Leading edge and strut bladders are the most common replacement parts for inflatable wings. We stock bladders for current Duotone and Fanatic wing models. For minor damage, a wing repair kit with adhesive patches handles small tears in the canopy without replacing the entire bladder.

Board Protection

Nose guards, tail pads, and rail tape protect your foil board from wing impacts during learning. Foil board repair kits patch dings quickly to prevent water ingress into the core.

FAQ

Q: How often do wing bladders need replacing?
A: With normal use, bladders last 2–3 seasons. Replace if the wing won't hold pressure overnight or you find persistent slow leaks.

Q: What leash length for wing foiling?
A: A coiled leash extending to 1.5–2m is standard. Too short restricts movement; too long creates tangles.

Q: Can I repair a torn wing canopy myself?
A: Small tears (under 5cm) can be patched with adhesive sail repair tape. Larger damage should be professionally repaired.

name: Wing Boards
meta_description: Buy wing foil boards — Fanatic, Duotone & more. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Wing Foil Boards — From Beginner to Advanced

The board is your platform in wing foiling — it determines how early you get on foil, how stable you feel during takeoff, and how confidently you can ride in chop. Our wing foil board range covers high-volume beginner boards through to compact expert shapes from Fanatic and Duotone.

Board Volume and Size

For learning, more volume equals more stability. A beginner at 80kg should start with a board around 110–130L and 75–85cm wide. This provides enough float to stand, pump, and get on foil without sinking the tail. As skills develop, riders move to smaller boards — 70–90L for intermediate and 40–60L for advanced riders who can waterstart directly onto the foil.

Construction Types

Wing foil boards come in two main constructions. Hardboards (carbon, fibreglass, or sandwich) offer the best performance — stiff, light, and responsive. Inflatable foil boards are more practical for travel and storage, though they sacrifice some stiffness and direct feel.

Fanatic's Sky Wing is our most recommended beginner board — wide, stable, with a flat deck and recessed foil track that positions the foil optimally. The Duotone Pace series covers intermediate to advanced riders with refined shapes for speed, manoeuvrability, and jumping.

Why Buy Wing Boards at Surf Store

We stock wing foil boards from both Fanatic and Duotone with expert advice on volume, width, and foil compatibility. Our team can match you with the right board for your weight, skill level, and riding goals.

FAQ

Q: Can I use a SUP board for wing foiling?
A: Some SUP boards accept foil mounts, but a dedicated wing foil board with a reinforced foil track performs significantly better and is safer.

Q: What board size for an 80kg intermediate wing foiler?
A: 75–90L gives a good balance of stability and manoeuvrability once you can consistently get on foil.

Q: Do I need a different board for kite foiling vs wing foiling?
A: Wing foil boards tend to be wider and higher volume than kite foil boards for the same rider weight. Some boards work for both, but dedicated shapes perform better.

name: Wing Foils
meta_title: Wing Foil Hydrofoils — Complete Sets & Parts | surf-store.com
meta_description: Buy wing foil hydrofoils — complete sets & parts. Duotone, Fanatic, F-One & more. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
meta_keywords: wing foil hydrofoil,wing foil set,foil mast,front wing
description_html:
Wing Foil Hydrofoils — Complete Sets & Individual Components

The hydrofoil is what makes wing foiling possible — lifting you above the water surface to glide with minimal drag. Our wing foil range includes complete foil sets for beginners and individual components for riders looking to tune their setup. We stock foils from Duotone, Fanatic, and select third-party brands.

Complete Foil Sets

A complete foil set includes everything you need: front wing, stabiliser (rear wing), mast, fuselage, and mounting hardware. For beginners, we recommend starting with a complete set from a single brand — components are matched and tested together, eliminating compatibility issues. The Duotone Spirit foil system and Fanatic Aero foil are our top picks for learning.

Choosing Your Foil Components

Front wing area determines lift — larger wings (1500–2000cm²) provide early liftoff and stable, slow-speed flight for learning. Smaller wings (800–1200cm²) offer higher top speed and more manoeuvrability for advanced riders. Mast length affects clearance above chop — start short (60–70cm) and progress to 85cm+ as technique improves. Fuselage length affects turning radius — shorter fuselages turn tighter.

Materials

Aluminium masts are durable and affordable — ideal for learning when crashes are frequent. Carbon masts are lighter, stiffer, and provide better energy transfer from pumping — worth the upgrade for intermediate and advanced riders. Front wings are typically carbon or carbon-fibreglass composite regardless of price level.

Why Buy Wing Foils at Surf Store

We build complete foil packages and can advise on wing area, mast length, and fuselage selection for your weight and skill level.

FAQ

Q: Are wing foils compatible across brands?
A: Not always — mast-to-board connections, fuselage bolt patterns, and wing attachment systems vary by brand. Mixing within a brand's system is safe.

Q: What front wing size for an 80kg beginner?
A: 1500–1800cm² provides easy liftoff and stable, forgiving flight while learning.

Q: How long does a foil mast last?
A: Aluminium masts last years with proper care. Carbon masts should be inspected for cracks after hard impacts — replace if structural damage is visible.

name: Wings
meta_description: Buy wing foil wings — Duotone, Fanatic, F-One & Cabrinha. All sizes. Free EU shipping from €99. Surf-store.com.
description_html:
Wing Foil Wings — All Sizes & Brands

The wing is your power source in wing foiling — it catches the wind and converts it into forward drive that lifts you onto the foil. Modern inflatable wings are remarkably light, stiff, and easy to handle compared to early designs. Our wing range includes models from Duotone, Fanatic, and Cabrinha in sizes from 2.5m to 7.0m+.

Wing Sizes Explained

Wing size determines how much power you generate. Match size to your weight and wind conditions:

  • 2.5–3.5m: Strong wind (25+ knots), lightweight riders, speed sailing
  • 4.0–5.0m: Medium wind (15–25 knots), the all-around sweet spot
  • 5.5–6.5m: Light wind (10–18 knots), heavier riders, learning
  • 7.0m+: Ultra-light wind, downwind cruising

Most riders at 75–85kg build a two-wing quiver: a 4.5m for powered conditions and a 6.0m for light-wind days.

Wing Design Features

Handle systems vary between brands — rigid boom handles, soft handles, or hybrid setups. Rigid handles provide better control at speed; soft handles are lighter and allow more hand positioning options. Window design affects visibility — larger windows help you see downwind during transitions. Canopy stiffness determines power stability — stiffer wings hold their shape better in gusts.

Our Top Picks

The Duotone Echo is our most recommended all-round wing — excellent relaunch, stable power, and suitable for all levels. The Duotone Unit D/LAB is the performance choice for experienced riders who want speed and upwind angle. The Duotone Slick is our beginner pick — lightweight and forgiving.

Why Buy Wings at Surf Store

We stock wings from 2.5m to 7.0m+ across multiple brands. Our team wing foils regularly and can advise on size, model, and handle configuration for your weight and conditions.

FAQ

Q: What wing size for a beginner at 80kg?
A: 5.5–6.0m gives enough power for learning in 12–20 knots without being overwhelming.

Q: How long do inflatable wings last?
A: With proper care (rinse, dry, store in bag), 2–4 seasons of regular use. Leading edge bladders are replaceable.

Q: Can I use a wing foil wing for land use?
A: Yes — wings work on skateboards, ice, snow, and inline skates. Just be aware of hard surface impact risks to the wing.
